steps.title: Review Title Surgical versus non-surgical treatment...
steps.instruction: Review Title Surgical versus non-surgical treatment for thoracolumbar burst fractures without neurological deficit: A systematic review and meta-analysis

steps.title: Review team members and their organisational affiliations Dr
steps.instruction: Review team members and their organisational affiliations Dr. Tzu-Yi Chou. School of Medicine, National Taiwan University Dr. Chung Liang Chai. Yee Zen General Hospital Dr. Fon-Yih Tsuang. National Taiwan University Hospital

steps.title: Patient reported outcome (PRO)
steps.instruction: Patient reported outcome (PRO): Visual Analog Score 0-100 mm (VAS) at 1 month

steps.title: PRO
steps.instruction: PRO: Oswestry Disability Index 0-100 % (ODI) at 1 month

steps.title: Searches We designed a search strategy...
steps.instruction: Searches We designed a search strategy using MEDLINE, EMBASE, Web of Science, and Google Scholar (with the key article of Wood 2003). Wood K, G Buttermann G, A Mehbod A, et al. Operative compared with nonoperative treatment of a thoracolumbar burst fracture without neurological deficit. A prospective, randomized study. Bone Joint Surg Am. 2003 May;85(5):773-81. doi: 10.2106/00004623-200305000-00001. *We did not restrict our searches in terms of language and publication year.

steps.title: Stage of review at time of this submission
steps.instruction: Stage of review at time of this submission. Review stage Started Completed 1 Preliminary searches Yes Yes 2 Piloting of the study selection process No No 3 Formal screening of search results against eligibility criteria No No 4 Data extraction No No 5 Risk of bias (quality) assessment No No 6 Data analysis No No

steps.title: Thoracolumbar fracture with A3 and A4 with N0 or N
steps.instruction: Thoracolumbar fracture with A3 and A4 with N0 or N

steps.title: Osteoporotic is the primary cause of...
steps.instruction: Osteoporotic is the primary cause of fracture, such as compression fracture. *We do not exclude study according to population age, sample size, and trauma severity. References:

steps.instruction: Catphuong Vu, David Gendelberg. Classifications in Brief: AO Thoracolumbar Classification System. Clin Orthop Relat Res (

steps.title: Intervention(s), exposure(s) Any surgical treatment (any...
steps.instruction: Intervention(s), exposure(s) Any surgical treatment (any internal instrument fixation systems), including percutaneous vertebroplasty. Timing: acute fracture within 4 weeks

steps.title: Comparator(s)/control
steps.instruction: Comparator(s)/control. Supportive care, such as bracing or pain medications with or without pain interventions. With no surgical intervention. Timing: acute fracture within 4 weeks

steps.title: Types of study to be included Included
steps.instruction: Types of study to be included Included: randomized controlled trials non-randomized studies (definition according to Cochrane Handbook Chapter 24) Excluded: population studies that are subject to ecological fallacy national database studies that are subject to ecological fallacy Studies with no control Studies other than English, Chinese, or languages that uninterpretable via Google Translate

steps.title: Context Tertiary spine institutions that can...
steps.instruction: Context Tertiary spine institutions that can provide surgical fixation of spine fractures.

steps.title: Data extraction (selection and coding) Dr
steps.instruction: Data extraction (selection and coding) Dr. Chou and Dr. Chai work independently on study selection according to the aforementioned inclusion and exclusion criteria. Dr. Chou and Dr. Chai work independently on extraction of data from included studies using a pre-piloted form. Where Dr. Tsuang will resolve disagreements and oversees the accuracy of the process.
